audit_objective: Exhaustive inventory of all repository-controlled paths capable of producing execution outcomes, authority creation, validation state transitions, or registry mutation outside the canonical governed spine.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_BLOCKED — invocation_nonce uniqueness enforced at execution gate
entries.bypass_risk: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY_SURFACE — bypass only if upstream governance prerequisites absent
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Execution eligibility requires prior authority, validated_object_hash, validation lineage, and replay reservation. Execution cannot succeed if any upstream governance prerequisite is absent.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_BLOCKED — invocation_nonce reserved at validation, consumed at execution
entries.bypass_risk: VALIDATION_BOUNDARY_SURFACE — bypass if identity drift between compile and validate succeeds
entries.determination: CANONICAL. sha256(canonical_aeo) recomputed and verified against stored compiled hash. Validation failure resolves to NULL. Identity continuity: compiled == validated enforced.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_BLOCKED — proof_registry uniqueness guard; proof_registry is append-only
entries.bypass_risk: FINALITY_BOUNDARY_SURFACE — bypass if proof can be emitted without execution lineage
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Proof requires execution lineage, validation lineage, and authority lineage. identity(validated) == identity(executed) == identity(proven) enforced. Proof without execution lineage resolves to NULL.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_NEUTRAL — compile creates identity reference; execution eligibility requires downstream validation
entries.bypass_risk: COMPILATION_SURFACE — identity established here; mutation between compile and validate is primary risk
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Produces canonical_aeo hash. Does not create execution eligibility; only creates identity anchor for downstream validation.

entries.replay_impact: PARTIAL — authority is append-only insert; status updates are mutable but bound
entries.bypass_risk: AUTHORITY_CREATION_SURFACE — authority created here; downstream must verify active status
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Authority creation requires session and continuity lineage. Authority state is MONITORED. Does not alone create execution eligibility.

entries.replay_impact: APPEND_ONLY — continuity_registry is append-only
entries.bypass_risk: PRECURSOR_SURFACE — creates legitimacy precursor; not execution-eligible alone
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Continuity precursor only. Requires downstream authority binding before any execution eligibility can be derived.

entries.replay_impact: APPEND_ONLY — session_registry is append-only
entries.bypass_risk: PRECURSOR_SURFACE — creates identity anchor; not execution-eligible alone
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Session identity precursor. Requires downstream continuity → authority before any execution eligibility.

entries.replay_impact: NONE — observability only; no mutation
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — observability_only_non_executable_non_authoritative_non_mutating
entries.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. These routes cannot authorize execution, create authority, or mutate legitimacy state. Escalation from GET to mutation resolves to OBSERVABILITY_MUTATION_ESCALATION → NULL.

entries.replay_impact: LINEAGE_ENFORCED — revocation and expiry are terminal; no fail-open path verified
entries.bypass_risk: LINEAGE_RESOLUTION_SURFACE — fail-open on registry lookup failure would be bypass
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Invalid lineage resolves to NULL. Fail-open condition (lookup failure → ACTIVE) not observed. Revocation, expiry, and parent mismatch all resolve to NULL.

entries.replay_impact: SESSION_BOUNDED — session expiry and continuity revocation both terminate eligibility
entries.bypass_risk: SESSION_ELIGIBILITY_SURFACE — session-state/continuity-state drift would be bypass
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Active session requires both active session record and active continuity lineage. ACTIVE session + INVALID continuity does not produce eligibility. Fail-open path not observed.

entries.replay_impact: BOOTSTRAP_BOUNDED — migrations create schema structure; no authority, proof finality, or execution capability created
entries.bypass_risk: SCHEMA_MUTATION_SURFACE — only if applied outside governed workflow (raw wrangler d1 apply); see C-003
entries.determination: BOOTSTRAP_ONLY. Migrations create schema tables and append-only triggers. They do not create authority state, execution eligibility, or proof records. Production apply path is governed_workflow_only. Raw production apply is DENIED per EXECUTION_SURFACES.json. Append-only triggers (e.g. bootstrap_sovereignty_registry) enforce mutation_capable=false, read_only=true at schema level.

entries.replay_impact: PARTIAL — historical state rewrite at migration time; PR-governed but not runtime-authority-bound
entries.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — historical proof dedupe/backfill can rewrite historical registry state; bounded by PR governance
entries.determination: BOOTSTRAP_ONLY. Proof deduplication and backfill migrations operate only at migration time under PR governance. They do not create execution eligibility or new authority. Residual risk: archive-before-delete semantics; no verified emergency rollback surface present.

entries.canonical_owner: UNBOUND — not scoped to governed workflow
entries.replay_impact: UNGOVERNED — migration replay semantics outside runtime replay protections
entries.bypass_risk: HIGH — OPEN: schema mutation without legitimacy evidence if applied out-of-band
entries.determination: BYPASS_CANDIDATE (pre-existing OPEN). Direct wrangler d1 migrations apply against production database bypasses governed workflow boundary. Classification: UNBOUND_DATABASE_WRITE → NULL. Pre-existing in BYPASS_PATHS.json and REGISTRY_MUTATION_SURFACE_INVENTORY.json. No new surface.

entries.replay_impact: PARTIAL — bootstrap ensures are idempotent schema guards; no execution eligibility created
entries.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — MONITORED: creates schema not authority; bootstrap path cannot escalate to execution eligibility
entries.determination: BOOTSTRAP_ONLY. Runtime bootstrap ensure functions create schema structure at worker startup. They do not create authority objects, execution eligibility, or proof records. Status: MONITORED.

entries.replay_impact: APPEND_ONLY — session_registry is append-only; no mutable status paths
entries.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — OPEN: precursor surface; not authority-bound at write time
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Append-only session creation. Does not confer execution eligibility alone. Pre-existing OPEN governance status on precursor binding.

entries.replay_impact: APPEND_ONLY — continuity_registry is append-only
entries.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — OPEN: precursor surface; not authority-bound at write time
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Append-only continuity creation. Does not confer execution eligibility alone. Pre-existing OPEN governance status on precursor binding.

entries.replay_impact: PARTIAL — authority is append-only insert; MONITORED
entries.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — MONITORED: creates authority state, but execution requires downstream validation
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Authority creation requires session and continuity lineage. MONITORED. Authority insert alone does not create execution eligibility.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_SAFE — status transitions are governed by prior canonical state; append-semantics enforced at constraint level
entries.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — MONITORED: mutable by design but bound to authority, validator, and proof state
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Mutable status transitions are fully bound to authority, validator, and proof state. MONITORED.

entries.replay_impact: PARTIAL — AEO identity anchor established here; downstream validation must verify
entries.bypass_risk: LOW — MONITORED
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Append-only AEO identity anchor write. Execution eligibility requires downstream /validate.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_SAFE — replay protection active at validation gate
entries.bypass_risk: LOW — MONITORED
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Append-only validation record. Bound to authority and identity continuity.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_SAFE — invocation_nonce reservation and consumption enforced
entries.bypass_risk: LOW — MONITORED: nonce reservation prevents replay
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Invocation nonce reserved at validation, consumed at execution. Mutable but replay-protected.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_SAFE — execution_registry.validated_object_hash UNIQUE; execution_registry.workflow_run_id UNIQUE
entries.bypass_risk: LOW — MONITORED: uniqueness constraints prevent execution replay
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Append-only execution record. Fully bound to authority, validation, and nonce constraints. Uniqueness guards prevent replay.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_SAFE — proof_registry.decision_hash UNIQUE; proof_registry.workflow_run_id UNIQUE; append-only trigger enforced
entries.bypass_risk: LOW — MONITORED: fully bound; proof without execution lineage resolves to NULL
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Append-only proof write. Fully bound to execution, validation, and authority lineage. Uniqueness and append-only constraints prevent replay and unauthorized proof emission.

entries.replay_impact: NONE — no active KV write path for legitimacy-critical state
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — CLOSED: no active KV legitimacy writes found
entries.determination: NON_EXECUTING. No active KV.put() write path for legitimacy-critical state identified in repository scan. Closure status: CLOSED.

entries.canonical_owner: N/A — zero imports across entire codebase
entries.replay_impact: NONE — unreachable from any production execution path
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — unreachable; gateway.js, server.js, worker.js all throw hard-disable on invocation; registry.js has zero imports in production src/
entries.determination: NON_EXECUTING. registry.js contains hardcoded demo credentials (MS-DEMO-001, demo-signature-v1) but is unreachable from any production path. gateway.js, server.js, and worker.js are all hard-disabled with throw statements. No production import chain exists. Demo artifact only.

entries.replay_impact: NONE — ReconciliationFlags.mutation_capable: false, creates_authority: false, creates_proof: false, remote_execution_legitimacy: false (compile-time constants)
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — mutation_capable hardcoded false; creates_authority hardcoded false
entries.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. ReconciliationFlags type enforces mutation_capable: false, creates_authority: false, creates_proof: false, remote_execution_legitimacy: false. Returns drift analysis only. Cannot create execution eligibility.

entries.replay_impact: NONE — Object.freeze() immutable snapshot; evidence_only: true as const
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — frozen immutable object; evidence_only enforced at type level
entries.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. Deterministic hash of frozen snapshot object. evidence_only: true as const. Cannot produce mutable state.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_SAFE — hash chain integrity enforced; each entry carries previous_entry_hash; mutation_allowed: false hardcoded
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — mutation_allowed: false, mutates_runtime_state: false, creates_authority: false all hardcoded in interface
entries.determination: NON_EXECUTING. Append-only in-memory telemetry pipeline. mutation_allowed: false and mutates_runtime_state: false are compile-time interface invariants. Cannot authorize execution or mutate legitimacy state.

entries.replay_impact: NONE — GET-only; writes are append-only evidence records
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — append-only evidence writes; non-authoritative; escalation resolves to NULL
entries.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. Federation reconcile routes are GET-only. Any side-effect writes are append-only evidence records to observability registries. Cannot produce execution eligibility or authority state.

entries.replay_impact: NONE — evidence generation only
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — read-only reporting surface
entries.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. Install base report generator. Evidence-only, non-authoritative, non-mutating.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_SAFE — invocation_nonce uniqueness; bounded by max_executions=1; rerun fails closed once authority/execution consumed
entries.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — MONITORED: all governance gates bound; workflow_dispatch inputs validated (decision_id, validated_object_hash, invocation_nonce)
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Sole production deployment surface. Traverses full canonical spine: /session → /continuity → /authority → /compile → /validate → /execute → /proof → governed wrapper → wrangler deploy. All governance prerequisites enforced.

entries.replay_impact: OPEN — can mint multiple deploy-ready (decision_id, validated_object_hash, invocation_nonce) tuples on repeated dispatch; nonce is single-use downstream
entries.bypass_risk: HIGH — OPEN (pre-existing): repeated dispatch creates fresh candidate deploy tuples; no workflow-level constraint on number of minted tuples
entries.determination: CANONICAL with pre-existing OPEN replay risk. Creates authority objects and invocation nonces for downstream governed-deploy.yml. Execution does not occur here. Pre-existing OPEN governance status on prepare-governed-deploy replay surface. No new surface.

entries.replay_impact: REPLAY_PROTECTED — duplicate release_id and release_tag overwrite both rejected in replay prevention step
entries.bypass_risk: LOW — evidence_only: true, creates_authority: false, creates_execution: false; no GitHub release created, no tags pushed, no code committed; audit artifacts only
entries.determination: NON_EXECUTING. Classifies and verifies release provenance. Produces evidence-only audit artifacts (release-provenance.json, proposed-registry-patch.json) that are uploaded as workflow artifacts but NOT committed to main. No deployment authority created. BREAK_GLASS releases fail closed and cannot be normalized to CANONICAL_RELEASE. Previously unclassified in WORKFLOW_DISPATCH_DEPLOYMENT_SURFACE_INVENTORY; now classified.

entries.replay_impact: IDEMPOTENT — duplicate proof_id check prevents re-appending existing proof entry; pull-rebase before commit prevents concurrent appends
entries.bypass_risk: LOW — MERGE_LEGITIMACY_VALID required before canonical proof emitted; stale review, reviewer mismatch, and scope coverage failures all resolve to NULL; direct push to main blocked by branch protection (GH013)
entries.determination: CANONICAL. Merge proof persistence surface. Proof emission requires MERGE_LEGITIMACY_VALID, reviewer legitimacy, exact-object admission, and scope coverage validation. Proof cannot be emitted for LEGITIMACY_NULL or UNKNOWN merge status. Appends only to merge_proof_registry.jsonl. Previously unclassified in WORKFLOW_DISPATCH_DEPLOYMENT_SURFACE_INVENTORY; now classified.

entries.replay_impact: NONE — permissions: contents: read; no write paths
entries.bypass_risk: NONE — CLOSED: runtime_mutation_capable: false, authority_created: false, deployment_performed: false verified by workflow assertions
entries.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. Read-only conformance check. Governance boundaries asserted at runtime (non_operative, authority_created=false, deployment_performed=false, runtime_mutation_capable=false, production_proof_emitted=false). Previously unclassified in WORKFLOW_DISPATCH_DEPLOYMENT_SURFACE_INVENTORY; now classified.

A_runtime_routes.notes: All runtime routes classified in prior audit phases.

B_lifecycle_helpers.notes: resolveContinuityLineage and activeSession classified in prior audit phases.

C_bootstrap_schema.notes: 61 migration files (0001-0061) classified as BOOTSTRAP_ONLY under governed workflow. wrangler_d1_cli_apply remains BYPASS_CANDIDATE (pre-existing OPEN). No new surfaces.

D_registry_mutation_helpers.notes: All registry writes route through canonical src/index.ts routes. registry.js is unreachable demo artifact (NON_EXECUTING). kv_mutation_surface CLOSED.

E_federation_ingestion.notes: All federation and ingestion surfaces are evidence-only. ReconciliationFlags and AppendOnlyTelemetryPipeline enforce mutation_capable: false at type level.

F_workflow_ci.notes: 3 workflows previously absent from WORKFLOW_DISPATCH_DEPLOYMENT_SURFACE_INVENTORY now classified. governed-release.yml: NON_EXECUTING (evidence-only). merge-proof.yml: CANONICAL (proof-persistence, MERGE_LEGITIMACY_VALID required). conformance.yml: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY (read-only). Pre-existing OPEN on prepare-governed-deploy replay risk remains.

G_observability.notes: All 10 observability surfaces from OBSERVABILITY_WRITE_SURFACE_INVENTORY confirmed evidence-only. No mutation escalation path. Observability != authority invariant holds.

governance_invariants_verified.federation_mutation_capable_false: VERIFIED — ReconciliationFlags.mutation_capable: false enforced at type level
governance_invariants_verified.telemetry_mutation_allowed_false: VERIFIED — AppendOnlyTelemetryPipeline.mutation_allowed: false enforced at interface level
governance_invariants_verified.bootstrap_creates_no_execution_eligibili: VERIFIED — migration creates no authority, proof finality, or execution capability per EXECUTION_SURFACES.json
governance_invariants_verified.legacy_demo_paths_disabled: VERIFIED — worker.js, gateway.js, server.js all throw on invocation; registry.js has zero production imports
governance_invariants_verified.governed_release_evidence_only: VERIFIED — creates_authority: false, creates_execution: false, no code committed, no GitHub releases pushed

closure_basis: All seven domains (A-G) have been exhaustively inventoried. unknown_surfaces = 0. All mutation-capable surfaces are classified. No new BYPASS_CANDIDATE surfaces were discovered. Pre-existing OPEN bypass candidates (wrangler_d1_cli_apply, raw_d1_migration, prepare-governed-deploy replay) remain classified and tracked but are not new discoveries. Three previously unclassified workflows (governed-release.yml, merge-proof.yml, conformance.yml) are now classified and confirmed to not introduce new bypass paths. The closure criteria from issue #1695 are satisfied: every mutation-capable path is classified, every execution-capable path is classified, no unresolved bypass candidates remain beyond pre-existing tracked OPEN items, and all identified paths reconcile to canonical governance ownership.

closure_criteria_verification.note: Pre-existing OPEN bypass candidates (C-003, raw_d1_migration, F-002 replay) are classified, not unresolved. They are tracked governance gaps, not unknown surfaces.
